title: The Blind Man at Jericho
subtitle: A beggar by the road cries out, and the Light passes by

stillness: Let the crowd of your thoughts grow quiet.
The throng of phantasies and wandering thoughts scatters the mind and troubles the voice of the heart in prayer.
Be still, and let Jesus draw near to the heart he would enlighten.
gospel: It came to pass, as the Lord Jesus drew near to Jericho,
that a certain blind man sat by the wayside, begging.
And hearing the crowd passing by, he asked what this might be.
They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing.
And he cried out, saying: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me.
And those who went before rebuked him, that he should hold his peace.
But he cried out the more: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me.
And Jesus, standing still, commanded him to be brought to him.
And he asked him, saying: What wilt thou that I do for thee?
And he said: Lord, that I may see.
And Jesus said to him: Receive thy sight; thy faith hath made thee whole.
And immediately he saw, and followed him, glorifying God.
And all the people, when they saw it, gave praise to God.
behold: Behold a blind man, seated by the road, begging.
He cannot see the One who passes; he hears only the crowd going by, and asks what it might be.
Perceiving that Jesus is passing, he begs for mercy with a cry that is not only outward but inward.
In calling 'Jesus, Son of David' he confesses the human nature, for from David the King he took flesh;
in calling 'Have mercy on me' he confesses the divine, for it belongs to God to have mercy on the creature he made.
Though the passing crowd rebukes him, all the more he cries out and begs for mercy,
fearing lest, by their rebuke, the hearing and the granting of his petition be hindered.
See here also the gentleness of the Lord, who did not disdain not only to stand still, but even to speak to a person poor and lowly.
He stood still, for the blind man could not follow him, and commanded him to be brought near,
that he who had first touched him by faith might now draw near also in the body.
consider: He who created you without you will not justify you without you.
Christ asked, 'What wilt thou that I do for thee?' — not because he did not know, but to draw out his confession.
For he does not heal the unwilling, but the willing; no salvation is true salvation if it is given to one who refuses it.
While the blind man cried only in general, 'Have mercy on me,' he was not yet healed;
but when he laid bare his own particular misery — 'Lord, that I may see' — then he was healed.
Learn from this that none can be healed of the soul's infirmity unless he confess.
And it is not enough to have received the light, unless one also follow the light, lest he walk in darkness.
Mystically, this blind man is the whole human race, which, knowing nothing of the brightness of the supernal light in its first parent, received the darkness of its own damnation.
Jericho is the moon, which, waning by its monthly turns, well signifies the failing of our mortality.
When our Maker drew near to Jericho, the blind man came back to the light: for when the Godhead took up the weakness of our flesh, the human race received the light it had lost.

Whoever knows not the brightness of the eternal light is blind; if he already believes in the Redeemer, he sits by the way; if he believes and prays to receive the eternal light, he sits by the way and begs.
And the more the crowd rebukes the blind man, the more he cries out — a sign that the more impediments meet us, the more we must cry out.
prayer: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me.
You who do not disdain the poor and the lowly, stand still for me.
Lay bare my misery before you, and grant me the light you would give out of your own goodness.
Let me not only receive the light, but follow you in the light, glorifying God,
lest I walk in darkness. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Quisquis ergo cæcitatis suæ tenebras agnoscit, quisquis hoc quod sibi deest lumen æternitatis intelligit, clamet medullis cordis, clamet et vocibus oris, dicens: Jesu, fili David, miserere mei.
latinJewel.english: Whoever therefore acknowledges the darkness of his own blindness, whoever understands that the light of eternity is wanting to him, let him cry out from the marrow of his heart, let him cry out also with the voice of his lips, saying: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me.
